footer.tagline: AI for Safer Cities and Safer Journeys
footer.description: NariRaksha is a gender-responsive AI initiative focused on women's safety, helping cities build secure, inclusive urban transit environments.
footer.newsletterSub: Subscribe to receive monthly safety insights & updates.

home.heroSubtitle: Building safer cities through responsible, multilingual, and gender-responsive AI.
home.whyMattersDesc: Safety is the foundation of freedom and equality. Our cities thrive when every woman, student, and night-shift worker can travel without fear.
home.howWorksSubtitle: Fusing urban data, community signals, and responsible AI to inform urban planning and transit decisions.
home.impactVisionSubtitle: Our targets for building secure, inclusive cities.
home.trustSubtitle: Safety cannot exist without trust. We are committed to absolute data privacy and ethical AI design.
home.feedbackSubtitle: Help us identify high-risk areas and design better safety features.

about.title: About NariRaksha
about.subtitle: Redefining urban safety with ethical AI and civic collaboration.
about.missionDesc: To empower women, communities, and transit planners with actionable, real-time safety insights using gender-responsive, responsible artificial intelligence.
about.visionDesc: A world where every city's transit network is designed with the safety, comfort, and dignity of women as a baseline standard.
about.whyExistsDesc: Traditional urban infrastructure often overlooks the unique safety challenges faced by women at night, in transit, and during daily commutes. NariRaksha bridges this gap by highlighting gender-specific risks and providing actionable recommendations.
about.p1Desc: Zero tracking of individual identities. We process aggregate signals to identify environmental risks.
about.p2Desc: Designed for all women, across socio-economic backdrops, supporting 6 regional languages.
about.p3Desc: AI recommendations are always vetted by urban planners and community leaders.

caseStudy.title: Case Study: Safe Journeys
caseStudy.subtitle: Deploying NariRaksha AI across model cities and public transit hubs.
caseStudy.summary1Text: NariRaksha AI was piloted across three major public transit routes. By analyzing lighting parameters, crowd densities, and historical citizen inputs, the platform predicted safety scores with 92% accuracy, leading to a 30% increase in security patrols and new lighting installations in high-risk zones.
caseStudy.summary3Text: Our pilot project integrated sensor data, lighting measurements, and voluntary commuter reports to evaluate routes for night-shift workers. Working alongside municipal agencies, we identified 14 dark zones and 8 transit gaps. The platform generated decision support reports, resulting in immediate budget allocation for smart streetlighting and night shuttle services.
caseStudy.summaryFullText: The comprehensive deployment of NariRaksha AI involved creating parallel pipelines for environmental feature extraction and community feedback processing. In collaboration with NGOs, we verified that incorporating qualitative commuter stories reduced algorithmic bias, leading to fairer resource distribution for suburban areas.
caseStudy.problemDesc: Urban planning decisions often rely on macro data that lacks gender segregation, leaving critical safety vulnerabilities unmapped.
caseStudy.solutionDesc: A localized, multilingual AI diagnostic tool that evaluates physical street safety indicators alongside community sentiment.
caseStudy.implementationDesc: Deployed as a light web-portal accessible in 6 languages, integrated with local government transit dashboards.
caseStudy.impactDesc: Over 45 high-risk zones revitalized with streetlights and patrol routes optimized using safety heatmap indicators.
caseStudy.lessonsDesc: Community trust is key. Anonymous reporting options significantly improved engagement, particularly among students.
caseStudy.futureDesc: Scaling NariRaksha to 10 more cities, integrating real-time transit schedule alerts and panic assistance support.

howWorks.title: How It Works
howWorks.subtitle: Discover the five-step process behind NariRaksha's gender-responsive safety AI.
howWorks.step1Desc: We map physical factors like street lighting quality, pedestrian infrastructure, and nearby public transport accessibility.
howWorks.step2Desc: AI models process localized parameters to identify patterns of vulnerabilities during dark and low-traffic hours.
howWorks.step3Desc: Commuters contribute anonymous reports about lighting issues, unsafe pockets, and harassment incidents.
howWorks.step4Desc: Transit agencies and municipalities receive priority heatmaps recommending infrastructure fixes and patrol updates.
howWorks.step5Desc: Collaborative action yields well-lit streets, active public spaces, and highly reliable transit journeys for women.

transparency.title: AI Transparency & Ethics
transparency.subtitle: We believe safety technology must be accountable, private, and explainable.
transparency.doesDesc1: Analyzes spatial parameters like street lighting, transit density, and crowd presence.
transparency.doesDesc2: Synthesizes community feedback to identify clusters of recurring safety concerns.
transparency.doesDesc3: Generates priority rankings for civic authorities to allocate lighting and security resources.
transparency.doesNotTitle: What NariRaksha AI Does NOT Do
transparency.doesNotDesc1: Does NOT track or store personally identifiable information (PII) or locations of individuals.
transparency.doesNotDesc2: Does NOT engage in surveillance or facial recognition technologies.
transparency.doesNotDesc3: Does NOT replace community reporting—it enhances it with spatial data.
transparency.privacyDesc: All commuter reports are completely anonymous. We aggregate data at the street level to ensure individual journeys cannot be tracked.
transparency.biasDesc: Algorithms are calibrated to avoid favoring high-income areas, ensuring resource allocation recommendations are equitable.
transparency.oversightDesc: No automated policing. AI outputs serve as guides for human urban planners and community safety coordinators.

communityPage.title: Community Portal
communityPage.subtitle: Share your commute insights anonymously and help us co-create safer city transit.
communityPage.a1Desc: Empowering daily commuters to point out dark alleys, unsafe bus stops, and transit safety gaps.
communityPage.a2Desc: Supporting students travelling to late-evening coaching, colleges, and libraries.
communityPage.a3Desc: Providing clarity on safe travel corridors and bus routing for school and college children.
communityPage.a4Desc: Ensuring night-shift employees in IT, health, and manufacturing have safer late-night transit connections.
communityPage.a5Desc: Equipping grassroots volunteers with empirical data to lobby for lighting and transit modifications.
communityPage.a6Desc: Fostering collective action to improve surveillance, street life, and neighborhood trust.

awarenessHub.title: Awareness Hub
awarenessHub.subtitle: Your guide to urban safety, digital safety, and responsible technology.

storiesPage.title: Stories of Safety & Journeys
storiesPage.subtitle: Illustrative journeys highlighting safety vulnerabilities and the impact of NariRaksha AI interventions.
storiesPage.journey1Desc: Meera commuted from her suburban tech-park office at 11:30 PM. The final 800 meters home were unlit, with zero transit visibility. After NariRaksha AI flagged this dark route, the municipality installed high-efficiency LED lights and scheduled night-patrol stops. Today, Meera walks home confidently.
storiesPage.journey2Desc: Anjali relied on public buses to return from late library sessions. A particular bus junction was notorious for lighting issues. Community feedback via NariRaksha prompted the local bus operator to reposition security and relocate the stop to a well-lit storefront location.
storiesPage.journey3Desc: Fathima traveled to early morning wholesale markets at 4:30 AM. Using local languages, she submitted feedback about dark transit terminals. The data-driven report compiled by NariRaksha AI convinced the transport authorities to activate terminal lighting earlier, benefiting hundreds of morning workers.
